name: Bob James
description: American jazz keyboardist, arranger, and record producer (born 1939)
abstract: Robert McElhiney James (born December 25, 1939) is an American jazz keyboardist, arranger, and record producer. He founded the band Fourplay and wrote "Angela", the theme song for the TV show Taxi.

about_quotes.speaker: Hubert Laws
about_quotes.evidence: George Benson – on that tour, George Benson, Bob James, Ron Carter, Jack Dejohnette... I remember it, because – Hank Crawford and Fred would get into it on the bus.
about_quotes.doc_title: Hubert Laws — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Alan Raph
about_quotes.evidence: Creed had Verve Records at that time. And every now and then he’d pull some new talent — not new but unknown, relatively unknown. And he pulled in Bob James and Don Sebesky around the same time, gave them both an album... Bob became an international star.
about_quotes.doc_title: Alan Raph interviewed by Monk Rowe, Danbury, Connecticut, November 20, 2001

about_quotes.speaker: Brubeck
about_quotes.evidence: And then Bob James got involved... So just by bad luck the first project he could kill to show Bob James who had more power, it was our record.

about_quotes.speaker: Junior Mance Part II
about_quotes.evidence: Bob James is another one that used it, great on the electric piano.
about_quotes.doc_title: Junior Mance Part II interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, January 18, 1999 [interview 2 of 2]

about_quotes.speaker: Harold Danko
about_quotes.evidence: they used Bob James and whoever was the house rhythm section.
about_quotes.doc_title: Harold Danko interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, April 28, 2021
